title: acer macrophyllum var. normalis
intro: Acer macrophyllum var. normalis, also known as the Oregon white oak, is a deciduous tree or shrub native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The Oregon white oak is said to have numerous health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. It can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its bark, leaves, and roots can be used in various herbal preparations.
